title: Iterative Preorder Traversal of Binary Tree

content.problemStatement:
You are given the level-order representation of a binary tree (with `null` representing missing nodes).
Your task is to print the **preorder traversal** (Root → Left → Right) of the binary tree.

Follow-upThe recursive approach is simple — can you implement it **iteratively** using a stack?

**Function Declaration****Function Name**preorderTraversalpreorderTraversalpreorderTraversal

**Parameters**
rootrootroot : A pointer/reference to the root node of the binary tree.
**Return Value**
Returns an **array of integers**.
Constraints:
1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
0≤N≤1000 \leq N \leq 1000≤N≤100
−100≤Node.val≤100-100 \leq \text{Node.val} \leq 100−100≤Node.val≤100
The input represents a valid binary tree structure.
content.outputFormat:
Input Format


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases.




For each test case:



The first line contains an integer NNN, the number of elements in the array.
The second line contains NNN elements representing the **level-order traversal** of the binary tree.
Use nullnullnull to denote a missing (non-existent) child.




Output Format
For each test case, print the preorder traversal of the tree as space-separated values.

solution.approach:
There are two common approaches to perform preorder traversal:

This is the most intuitive method.


Visit the current node.
Recursively traverse the left subtree.
Recursively traverse the right subtree.
**Algorithm:**


If the current node is `null`, return.
Add the current node’s value to the result.
Recur for the left child.
Recur for the right child.
**Time Complexity:**
`O(N)` — each node is visited once.

**Space Complexity:**
`O(H)` — due to recursive call stack (where `H` is the height of the tree).

The recursive method is simple but can cause stack overflow for deep trees.
An iterative approach using an explicit **stack** avoids recursion.

**Algorithm:**



Create an empty stack and push the root node.




While the stack is not empty:



Pop the top node.
Record its value.
Push the right child (if it exists).
Push the left child (if it exists).



The recorded values form the preorder traversal.



**Reason for pushing right first:**
Since the stack is **LIFO**, pushing the right child first ensures the left child is processed first, maintaining preorder order (Root → Left → Right).

**Time Complexity:**
`O(N)` — every node is visited exactly once.

**Space Complexity:**
`O(N)` — stack may hold up to all nodes in worst case (skewed tree).
